name: Merge Two Sorted Arrays (Parallel AND Example)
description: Example flowchart showing a parallel AND (fork-join) structure. Two sorted arrays A and B are processed in parallel (e.g., validated or prepared). Both paths must complete before the merge step can proceed. The diamond node "Both ready?" has two incoming edges—one from each parallel path—and one outgoing edge to the merge. This is the classic converging-AND pattern: two arrows into a decision node, one arrow out.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A1["Sorted arrays A B"]
    B1["Prepare/validate A"]
    C1["Prepare/validate B"]
    D1{"Both ready?"}
    E1["Merge A and B\ncompare fronts append smaller"]
    F1["Return merged sorted array"]
    A1 --> B1
    A1 --> C1
    B1 --> D1
    C1 --> D1
    D1 -->|Yes: parallel AND| E1
    E1 --> F1
    classDef red fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff,stroke:#c0392b
    classDef yellow fill:#ffd43b,color:#000,stroke:#f59f00
    classDef green fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef lightblue fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    classDef violet fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef lavender fill:#e6e6fa,color:#333,stroke:#b19cd9
    class A1 red
    class B1,C1 green
    class E1 green
    class F1 violet
    class D1 lavender
